rows.description: Recepta varia
rows.notes: Clear centered page title, fully legible and no monetary figures on this line.

rows.description: Novr. 10 1774 Dr. Shortridge's Benef: half a year to Midr.
rows.notes: Description and amounts very clear. Columns aligned with pounds=100, shillings=0, pence=0; vertical ruling unambiguous.

rows.description: Novr. 22 Mr Bloomfields Plate Money
rows.notes: Clear reading of '08 0 0' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; interpreted as £8 0s 0d. No ambiguity.

rows.description: 1775 Jan: 30 Exchequer Annuity to Xmas
rows.notes: Amounts clearly written as '50 0 0' and align with the three vertical columns.

rows.description: Midsr. & Xmas Div. in stock, 3 pr Cent: consol.:
rows.notes: Clear numeric '15 8 4' in the three monetary columns; handwriting legible and validates (s < 20, d < 12).

rows.description: Mar: 20 Increment, of Mr Richards, Bursar
rows.notes: Numbers read as '05 5 9' and align well with column lines; no ambiguity.

rows.description: Apr: 3 Took out of the Chest for L. Wittenham Div.:
rows.notes: Large, clear '200 0 0' visible; alignment with pounds column unambiguous.

rows.description: Jun: 5 Two Seances for Bac: Disp.:
rows.notes: Amount reads '01 1 0' clearly; description text and date legible.

rows.description: Dr. Shortridge's Benef: half a year to Xmas
rows.notes: Duplicate-style entry matching earlier Shortridge line; amounts '100 0 0' clearly aligned.

rows.description: Mr Oswald Jackson's Acct. for the Merton estate for the year from Mich: 1773 to Mich: 1774
rows.notes: Centered multi-line account header. Text is legible though slightly smaller; clearly introduces the following grouped entries.

rows.description: Money Rent
rows.notes: Clear amount '205 - 0 - 0' aligned under pounds/shillings/pence. No ambiguity.

rows.description: York Rent
rows.notes: Numeric shown as '007 - 2 - 0' — read as £7 2s 0d. Columns and digits clear.
Underlined sum '272 - 2 - 0' beneath the two rent lines. No descriptive text on the same line; numeric alignment is clear.

rows.description: Allowed Land Tax
rows.notes: Clear reading of '24 - 17 - 3' in the columns. All values within valid ranges.

rows.description: Rent for 3 Closes
rows.notes: Main digits '16 - 7 - 10' are clear. A small additional mark after the pence column was interpreted as a halfpenny (1/2) because (a) the mark resembles a small '1/2' or loop and (b) arithmetic with the following 'Deduct' and 'Rec'd Balance' lines requires a 1/2d to reconcile to the stated balances (see notes for Deduct and Rec'd Balance). Slight ambiguity from the small script fraction, but contextual arithmetic supports 0.5d.

rows.description: Deduct
rows.notes: Numbers read as '41 - 5 - 1' with a faint fractional mark after the pence that aligns with the halfpenny seen on the 'Rent for 3 Closes' line. Arithmetic: Allowed Land Tax (24 17 3) + Rent for 3 Closes (16 7 10 1/2) => 41 5 1 1/2, matching a deduction containing a 1/2d. Fraction inferred from column-matching and the clear Rec'd Balance line; fraction mark itself is faint.

rows.description: July 22? - Rec'd Balance
rows.notes: Right-hand numeric clearly reads '230 16 10 1/2' (pence shown with a clear '1/2'). Date 'July 22?' is slightly uncertain (the day marker has a question-like flourish), so confidence lowered slightly for description but amounts are clear and reconcile with the arithmetic: 272 2 0 minus 41 5 1 1/2 = 230 16 10 1/2.

rows.description: July 26 Increment from Mr Newbery, Bursar
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings/pence read as '55 10 2'. A small trailing fractional mark after the pence is present and resembles '3q' or a three‑quarters mark; interpreted as three farthings (0.75d). Fraction is legible but small; context (final subtotals include 3/4d) supports this reading.

rows.description: Aug. 17 Took out of the College Chest
rows.notes: Clear '27 11 6' in the three monetary columns; handwriting and column alignment unambiguous.

rows.description: Jul. 22 House Fees from Mr Savande, Dean
rows.notes: Monetary amount '13 10 0' is clear. The payor name is less certain — the manuscript looks like 'Savande' (or possibly 'Sarande'/'Surrande'); transcribed as it appears but with lower confidence on the name due to handwriting ambiguity.

Underlined subtotal reads '827 3 8 3/4' — pounds/shillings/pence (8) plus a clear fraction marking interpreted as three farthings (0.75d). The fraction mark is visible and consistent with the fractional marks used elsewhere on the page.

rows.description: Recepta pro hoc anno & pro debitis anni præcedentis
rows.notes: Final total at foot of page clearly written as '1575 6 00' next to the Latin heading. Text and numbers legible; slight uncertainty only from ornate script on Latin words, amounts are clear.
